data.body:
Correction: prior comment on this ticket was malformed (CLI syntax error — used --body as a flag instead of positional). Disregard it.

Proposed scope addition (2026-04-26): structural sentinel as the enforcement layer beneath the existing prose-level execution-trace SCs.

Today /dso:brainstorm phases/approval-gate.md gained a one-line SCRUTINY attestation HARD-GATE (cross_epic and pipeline sub-agent IDs, or SKIPPED with reason + user authorization). That blocks the most common rationalization path (orchestrator drafts approval prose without dispatching the scrutiny pipeline), but the attestation values are still orchestrator-authored — there is no machine check that the named sub-agent IDs correspond to real dispatches.

Suggested SC to absorb here:

> Structural sentinel for fidelity / scrutiny attestation. Brainstorm approval gate (phases/approval-gate.md) and implementation-plan plan-review gate read a sentinel file (e.g., <artifacts-dir>/scrutiny-attestation-<epic-id>.json) written only by the dispatched scrutiny / fidelity / cross-epic-interaction-classifier sub-agents themselves, containing sub-agent dispatch ID, timestamp, and reviewer dimensions covered. The SCRUTINY attestation line in approval-gate.md is verified against the sentinel by a pre-prompt-render check; orchestrator-authored attestation values without a matching sentinel are rejected. This converts the prose-level execution-trace SCs in this epic into a machine-checkable structural gate.

Why here vs. a new epic: this epic's existing SCs already target 'fidelity reviewers include an execution trace requirement' and 'completion verifier includes execution-based verification steps' — both prose-level. The sentinel is the structural enforcement underneath the same intent. Approach is TBD on this epic, so absorption is clean.

Adjacent but distinct: e884-d0ad (PostToolUse hook for sub-agent attention loss) shares hook infrastructure but targets a different failure mode (orchestrator going idle after Task returns, not gate enforcement). Worth a relates_to link if both proceed.

Context for why this surfaced now: during a /dso:brainstorm session on c183-ed2a (adversarial review of inference-sourced decisions), the orchestrator (Claude) skipped the scrutiny pipeline entirely while drafting the approval gate, surfaced the skip transparently, and almost wrote the ticket. User caught it and asked for a skill edit that would have prevented the rationalization. The one-line HARD-GATE was applied to approval-gate.md as the cheap fix. This SC is the structural complement.
